questions.question: How does a Custom Agent differ from Notion AI in terms of permissions?

options.text: Notion AI has its own permissions, while Custom Agents respect the user's permissions.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Notion AI respects your personal permissions and only sees what you can see. Custom Agents act as specialized team members with their own permissions.

options.text: Custom Agents act as specialized team members with their own permissions, while Notion AI acts as you.
options.explanation: This is correct. Notion AI acts as you and respects your personal permissions. Custom Agents act as specialized team members with their own permissions.

options.text: Notion AI is autonomous and can run in the background, unlike Custom Agents.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Custom Agents are autonomous and can run automatically in the background, whereas Notion AI is on-demand.

options.text: Custom Agents require the creator to have Full Access to the workspace to function.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While specific access is needed for resources, the agent itself operates independently of the creator's broader workspace permissions.

questions.question: When you create a new Custom Agent, what is the default level of access it has to pages in the workspace?

options.text: Access to all pages in the workspace.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. By default, new agents have no access to all pages in the workspace.

options.text: Access inherited from the creator of the agent.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. There are no inherited permissions. The agent can only access resources you explicitly give it permission to use.

options.text: No access to any pages or databases.
options.explanation: This is correct. Creators must explicitly enable access and accept a warning modal.

options.text: Access to public pages only.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. There is no distinction between public and private pages regarding default access; the default is strictly no access.

questions.question: Which permission level allows a user to view and modify the agent's instructions?

options.text: Can view and interact
options.explanation: This is incorrect. 'Can view and interact' allows read-only access to agent settings.

options.text: Can edit
options.explanation: This is correct. 'Can edit' includes the ability to view and modify agent instructions.

options.text: Full access
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While 'Full access' grants everything in 'Can edit', 'Can edit' specifically covers modifying instructions without needing the broader power to delete the agent.

options.text: Workspace Admin
options.explanation: This is incorrect. 'Workspace Admin' is a role, not a specific permission level for sharing the agent itself.

questions.question: What happens to a Custom Agent if the owner leaves the workspace and no one takes ownership for 7 days?

options.text: The agent is automatically deleted by the system.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The agent stops running, but is not necessarily deleted immediately.

options.text: The agent becomes private and inaccessible to the team.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The agent stops running, but it remains in the system.

options.text: The agent stops running.
options.explanation: This is correct. After 7 days of no ownership, the agent stops running.

options.text: The agent transfers ownership to the Workspace Admin.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The agent stops running rather than transferring ownership.

questions.question: How can a page owner stop a Custom Agent from accessing their specific page without contacting the agent creator?

options.text: Ask the agent to remove itself from the share menu.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Agents cannot remove themselves; the owner must do it.

options.text: Remove the page from the agent's Tools and Access settings.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. To remove an agent from a resource, you must use the share menu on the resource page, not the agent's settings.

options.text: Use the Agent Directory to delete the agent.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While admins can delete agents via the Directory, a standard page owner uses the share menu.

options.text: Remove the agent from the share menu of the page.
options.explanation: This is correct. As a page or database owner, you can remove any Custom Agent from your resource directly through the share menu.

questions.question: What is a key limitation regarding guests and restricted members using Custom Agents?

options.text: They cannot create new agents.
options.explanation: This is correct. Guests and restricted members cannot create Custom Agents.

options.text: They cannot view the output agents have produced.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. They can view the output produced in pages or databases shared with them.

options.text: They cannot make edits to pages.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. They can make edits to pages, which may trigger agents to run.

options.text: They cannot ask questions and receive answers.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. They can ask questions, provided the agent has access to the resources they need.

questions.question: Where can an Enterprise workspace admin find a full history of all Custom Agent actions?

options.text: AI Analytics
options.explanation: This is incorrect. AI Analytics identifies most-used and unused agents, but does not provide a full history of actions.

options.text: Agent Directory
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The Agent Directory is for viewing agents and managing permissions, not for logging action history.

options.text: Audit Log
options.explanation: This is correct. The Audit Log provides a full history of all Custom Agent actions.

options.text: Content Admin Search
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Content Admin Search is used to see what resources an agent can access, not for history.

questions.question: What specific permission level is required to grant a Custom Agent 'Can edit' access to a Notion resource?

options.text: View access on that resource.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. View access allows you to grant 'Can view' to the agent, but not 'Can edit'.

options.text: Full Access to the workspace.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. You must have Full Access to the specific resource, not necessarily the whole workspace.

options.text: Full Access to the resource.
options.explanation: This is correct. To grant any greater level of access beyond view, you must have Full Access to share that resource.

options.text: Can view and interact permission.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. This permission level is for sharing the agent with users, not for granting access to resources within the agent.

questions.question: When a Custom Agent retrieves information, which permissions does it use to access database rows?

options.text: The permissions of the person who triggered the agent.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The agent responds using its own access settings, not the permissions of the user.

options.text: The permissions of the agent creator.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The agent uses its own configured access, not the creator's.

options.text: The agent's own access settings.
options.explanation: This is correct. A Custom Agent responds using the agent’s own access, not the permissions of the person who triggered it.

options.text: Inherited permissions from the database owner.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. There are no inherited permissions.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the 'Content Admin Search' feature in the Enterprise plan?

options.text: Restrict who can create agents.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. This is the function of 'Creation controls'.

options.text: Identify most-used and unused agents.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. This is the function of 'AI Analytics'.

options.text: See what pages and databases each agent can access.
options.explanation: This is correct. Content Admin Search allows admins to see access scopes and manage permissions.

options.text: Transfer ownership of agents.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Ownership transfer is handled via the Members tab.
